behaviors.description: Primary translator output is grammatically coherent and preserves source meaning (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: it verifies translation quality/meaning preservation of neural MT output. Hypothetical elsewhere: API routing, retries, fallback wiring, DB access. LLM-DEP is explicit: correctness depends on real model output quality and variability; deterministic stubs can mask failures. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text focuses on output quality ("grammatically coherent", "preserves meaning"), not real network/OS failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Run real translation requests against the production-like MT model for a curated multilingual set with semantic/fluency acceptance checks (human rubric or strong semantic scorer), and fail when outputs become literal-but-wrong or ungrammatical.

behaviors.description: On primary API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and does not error to caller.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: error handoff between primary translator client and fallback module, plus caller-facing response handling. Hypothetical elsewhere: real network timeout behavior, MT output quality. MOCK-MISS applies at module boundary (primary client -> fallback path). Integration-brake: (a) no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess; a controlled 503 response from an in-process fake client reproduces this failure. (b) no dependence on AI output quality. (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior specifies status handling ("returns a 503 error") and transparency, not transport-layer/network phenomena.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + primary-client adapter + fallback translator; force primary adapter to return 503; assert successful response body comes from fallback and API layer returns non-error status.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: query/read correctness against SQLite-backed phrase store. Hypothetical elsewhere: primary API behavior, quality comparison pipeline. Failure boundary is module wiring (fallback translator <-> SQLite repo/schema), not one pure function. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond local DB access; in-process SQLite test DB is sufficient. (b) no AI output dependency. (c) no multi-step agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior explicitly targets "stored in a local SQLite database" lookup correctness, not OS-level file event/process/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real test SQLite database (temp file or in-memory), seed known phrase mappings, execute fallback translation calls, and assert exact stored phrase is returned for existing entries.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: algorithm result plus persistence side effect (metric storage). Hypothetical elsewhere: real MT quality, API outage handling. Unit-only test of edit-distance math misses storage/wiring failures; behavior explicitly requires both compute and store. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed; local/in-memory DB suffices. (b) no dependence on real AI output quality, only provided strings. (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text validates deterministic metric computation/storage, not quality of model-generated content.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke comparison flow with fixed primary/fallback strings, assert computed edit distance matches expected value, and verify metric row/event is persisted with correct identifiers.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison asynchronously.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: prioritization (primary chosen) plus background logging trigger for comparison. Hypothetical elsewhere: true MT semantic quality, network instability, multi-agent orchestration. Failure appears in module interaction (request path + async logging component). Integration-brake: (a) no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess; in-process async executor/queue can expose ordering/trigger bugs. (b) no requirement to judge AI output quality, only source selection and side-effect emission. (c) not multiple agentic steps. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior describes one service decision plus one background side effect, not a chained multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translators stubbed as available and deterministic; assert response uses primary output immediately, then await/inspect background sink to confirm comparison log/metric was emitted.
